# DevTools — Complete Tool Documentation

> A suite of 21 free, open-source, privacy-first developer tools at https://devtls.vercel.app, built with React and TypeScript. All processing happens client-side in your browser — no signup, no tracking, no data ever sent to a server. Source: https://github.com/venki0552/devtools (MIT license). Tool index: https://devtls.vercel.app/llms.txt

Each tool below lives at https://devtls.vercel.app followed by its route (e.g. /json → https://devtls.vercel.app/json).

## JSON Parser & Formatter (/json)
Intelligent JSON parser that handles: valid JSON, single-quoted JSON, trailing commas, unquoted keys, JS object literals, URL-encoded JSON, Base64-encoded JSON, backtick-wrapped JSON, JSON with comments, escaped JSON strings. Formats with configurable indentation (2/4/tab), key sorting, minification. Includes collapsible tree view with type badges, search, and clipboard support.

## XML Formatter (/xml)
Format, minify, and validate XML documents. Handles HTML fragments, missing closing tags, unescaped entities. Converts XML to JSON. Includes structure tree with namespace summary and attribute inspector.

## SQL Formatter (/sql-formatter)
Client-side SQL formatting using sql-formatter. Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, SQL Server, BigQuery dialects. Configurable keyword case (UPPER/lower/Capitalize), indentation, clause separation. Preserves comments, quoted identifiers, and string literals.

## GraphQL Formatter (/graphql)
Format GraphQL queries and mutations. Explore SDL schemas with type tree, field inspection, and reverse references. Validate query variables against schema definitions.

## CSV ↔ JSON Converter (/csv-json)
Bidirectional CSV/JSON conversion. Auto-detects delimiter (comma, tab, semicolon, pipe). Type inference for numbers, booleans, nulls. Handles nested JSON flattening with dot notation. Drag-and-drop file support.

## YAML ↔ JSON Converter (/yaml-json)
Bidirectional YAML/JSON conversion using js-yaml. Handles multi-document YAML, anchors/aliases, custom tags. Round-trip validation to detect type coercions. Configurable string quoting and flow style.

## Base64 Encode/Decode (/base64)
Encode/decode text and files. Supports Standard, URL-safe, and MIME Base64 variants. Auto-detects Base64 on paste. File mode with drag-and-drop, data URI generation, and image preview.

## JWT Decoder (/jwt)
Decode and inspect JWTs client-side. Color-coded token sections (header/payload/signature). Standard claim labels with human-readable timestamps. Expiry status with countdown. Algorithm security badges. Nested JWT detection.

## URL Encode/Decode (/url)
Encode/decode URLs with component, full URL, and form encoding modes. Query string parser with editable parameter table. URL builder with live preview. Handles double-encoding, IDN hostnames, IPv6 addresses.

## Hash Generator (/hash)
Generate MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512 hashes using Web Crypto API. Text and file input modes. Live hashing with debounce. Hash comparison mode for integrity verification. Output in hex or Base64.

## Regex Tester (/regex)
Test regex patterns with live match highlighting in Monaco editor. Capture group inspection. Replace mode with $1/$2 substitution. Common pattern library. Catastrophic backtracking detection via Web Worker timeout. Optional AI pattern explanation (bring your own Anthropic API key).

## CRON Builder (/cron)
Visual cron expression builder with cell grids for each field. Human-readable translation via cronstrue. Next 10 run times with timezone conversion. Supports 5-field standard, 6-field Quartz, and AWS EventBridge formats.

## HTTP Status Codes (/http-status)
Searchable reference for all HTTP status codes (100-511 + unofficial). Filterable by family (1xx-5xx). Expandable cards with descriptions, common causes, recommended actions, related headers, and code examples.

## Epoch Converter (/epoch)
Bidirectional epoch/datetime conversion with live clock. Auto-detects seconds vs milliseconds. Parses ISO 8601, US/EU formats, natural language, relative time. Timezone comparator for up to 5 zones with DST detection.

## UUID Generator (/uuid)
Generate UUIDv4, UUIDv7, and ULID. Bulk generation (up to 1000) with multiple output formats (one-per-line, JSON array, SQL VALUES). UUID decoder showing version, variant, and encoded timestamp (v1/v7).

## Color Converter (/color)
Convert between HEX, RGB, HSL, HSV, CMYK, Lab, LCH, Oklch, and CSS named colors. WCAG contrast checker against black/white. Palette generator with tints, shades, and color harmonies. Export as CSS variables or Tailwind config.

## Env Var Manager (/env)
Manage environment variables across multiple projects. Import from .env, shell exports, Docker, JSON, YAML. Export to .env, Docker flags, JSON, GitHub Actions, Kubernetes Secrets. Variable masking, grouping, and cross-project comparison.

## JSON Diff Viewer (/json-diff)
Deep diff two JSON objects using jsondiffpatch. Tree view with added/removed/changed/unchanged nodes. Flat list mode with dot-notation paths. Copy as JSON Patch (RFC 6902). Array diffing by index or identity key.

## Text Diff Viewer (/diff)
Compare two texts with unified or side-by-side diff using jsdiff. Word, line, character, and sentence diff algorithms. Ignore whitespace/case options. Configurable context lines. Export as unified diff or .patch file.

## SQL Visualizer (/sql-visualizer)
Client-side SQL analysis using node-sql-parser — no API calls. Generates: plain English summary, table/join graph (SVG), Venn diagrams per JOIN, data flow stepper, output shape table, filter/grouping analysis, potential performance issues.

## Mock API Generator (/mock-api)
Generate realistic mock data from JSON Schema, example JSON, or plain English descriptions — entirely in the browser using Faker. Locale-aware (US/UK/DE/FR/JP/IN). Export as JSON, NDJSON, CSV. Copy as fetch mock or MSW handler.

## Privacy & Security
All tools process data entirely in your browser using client-side JavaScript. No data is transmitted to any server. The only exception is the optional "Explain" tab in the Regex Tester: if (and only if) you provide your own Anthropic API key, the regex pattern is sent directly from your browser to the Anthropic API. The key is stored in localStorage and never touches any other server.
